2026-04-17T12:45:16.5382415Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-17T12:45:16.5382830Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-17T12:45:16.5383185Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-17T12:45:16.5383487Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-17T12:45:16.5383728Z [36;1m[0m

Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"issue_number":{"description":"Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"state_reason":{"description":"The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.","enum":["COMPLETED","NOT_PLANNED","DUPLICATE"],"type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"close_issue"},{"description":"Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_comment"},{"description":"Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. 2026-04-17T12:46:39.7903854Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-17T12:46:22.352397161Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"github","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"issue_number":"58288","method":"get","owner":"Azure","repo":"azure-sdk-for-net"},"name":"issue_read"}}}
2026-04-17T12:46:39.7933853Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-17T12:46:22.693236876Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58288,\"title\":\"[Mgmt Generator] PropertyFlatten drops Nullable wrapper on required value-type properties flattened from optional properties?: parent\",\"body\":\"## Problem\\n\\nIn [`FlattenPropertyVisitor.PropertyFlatten`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/Visitors/FlattenPropertyVisitor.cs#L650-L702), a required value-type property on an **optional** inner `properties?:` model is flattened as a **non-nullable** property on the outer model, which is unsafe (the getter will `NullReferenceException` if the inner `Properties` backing field is null) and inconsistent with the companion `SafeFlatten` code path, which correctly wraps such properties in `Nullable`.\\n\\n## Scenario (from Compute)\\n\\nTypeSpec:\\n\\n```tsp\\nmodel ProxyResourceCommunity extends PirCommunityGalleryResource {\\n  properties?: Properties;                     // OPTIONAL parent\\n}\\n\\nmodel CommunityGalleryImage\\n  is ProxyResourceCommunity { ... }\\n\\nmodel CommunityGalleryImageProperties {\\n  osType: OperatingSystemTypes;                // REQUIRED value-type child\\n  osState: OperatingSystemStateTypes;          // REQUIRED value-type child\\n  endOfLifeDate?: utcDateTime;                 // OPTIONAL child\\n  hyperVGeneration?: HyperVGeneration;         // OPTIONAL child\\n  ...\\n}\\n```\\n\\nGenerated (incorrect):\\n\\n```csharp\\npublic partial class CommunityGalleryImageData : ...\\n{\\n    internal CommunityGalleryImageProperties Properties { get; }   // may be null\\n\\n    // BUG: required value-type in optional parent - generated non-nullable, NRE risk\\n    public SupportedOperatingSystemType OSType =\\u0026gt; Properties.OSType;\\n    public OperatingSystemStateType OSState =\\u0026gt; Properties.OSState;\\n\\n    // OK: optional child - already nullable at the inner level\\n    public DateTimeOffset? EndOfLifeOn =\\u0026gt; Properties.EndOfLifeOn;\\n    public HyperVGeneration? HyperVGeneration =\\u0026gt; Properties.HyperVGeneration;\\n}\\n```\\n\\nExpected:\\n\\n```csharp\\npublic SupportedOperatingSystemType? OSType =\\u0026gt; Properties?.OSType;\\npublic OperatingSystemStateType?      OSState =\\u0026gt; Properties?.OSState;\\n```\\n\\nThis also matches the old AutoRest SDK\\u0026#39;s contract (e.g. `Azure.ResourceManager.Compute` historically exposed these as `Nullable`), so the bug is a breaking ApiCompat regression across every `properties?:` + required-value-type-child combination in the compute migration.\\n\\n## Root cause\\n\\n`PropertyFlatten` computes `isOverriddenValueType` but **never applies it** to the flattened property\\u0026#39;s type:\\n\\n```csharp\\n// FlattenPropertyVisitor.cs:650-702 (PropertyFlatten)\\nprivate void PropertyFlatten(ModelProvider model, ModelProvider propertyModel, ...)\\n{\\n    // dead: this tuple-list is declared but never populated\\n    var flattenedProperties = new List\\u0026lt;(bool IsOverriddenValueType, PropertyProvider FlattenedProperty)\\u0026gt;();\\n\\n    foreach (var innerProperty in innerProperties)\\n    {\\n        ...\\n        // computed but never consumed\\n        var isOverriddenValueType = innerProperty.Type.IsValueType \\u0026amp;\\u0026amp; !innerProperty.Type.IsNullable;\\n        var flattenedProperty =\\n            new FlattenedPropertyProvider(\\n                innerProperty.Description,\\n                innerProperty.Modifiers,\\n                innerProperty.Type,                  // \\u0026lt;-- BUG: should use WithNullable when isOverriddenValueType\\n                ...);\\n        ...\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\nCompare to the sibling `SafeFlatten` at [lines 739-744](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/Visitors/FlattenPropertyVisitor.cs#L739-L744), which applies it correctly:\\n\\n```csharp\\nvar isOverriddenValueType = innerProperty.Type.IsValueType \\u0026amp;\\u0026amp; !innerProperty.Type.IsNullable;\\nvar flattenedProperty =\\n    new FlattenedPropertyProvider(\\n        innerProperty.Description,\\n        innerProperty.Modifiers,\\n        isOverriddenValueType ? innerProperty.Type.WithNullable(true) : innerProperty.Type,\\n        ...);\\n```\\n\\nThe `IsOverriddenValueType` helper is also correctly consumed for **model factory method parameters** at [line 273](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/Visitors/FlattenPropertyVisitor.cs#L273):\\n\\n```csharp\\nif (IsOverriddenValueType(flattenedProperty))\\n{\\n    updatedParameter.Update(type: updatedParameter.Type.WithNullable(true));\\n}\\n```\\n\\nSo model factory parameters are generated as `Nullable` but the corresponding flattened property is generated as `T`. The types are internally inconsistent within a single generated model.\\n\\n## Proposed fix\\n\\nApply the same `WithNullable(true)` transform in `PropertyFlatten` that `SafeFlatten` already does:\\n\\n```diff\\n-                var isOverriddenValueType = innerProperty.Type.IsValueType \\u0026amp;\\u0026amp; !innerProperty.Type.IsNullable;\\n+                var isOverriddenValueType = innerProperty.Type.IsValueType \\u0026amp;\\u0026amp; !innerProperty.Type.IsNullable;\\n                 var flattenedProperty =\\n                     new FlattenedPropertyProvider(\\n                         innerProperty.Description,\\n                         innerProperty.Modifiers,\\n-                        innerProperty.Type,\\n+                        isOverriddenValueType ? innerProperty.Type.WithNullable(true) : innerProperty.Type,\\n                         flattenPropertyName,\\n                         ...);\\n```\\n\\nThe getter bodies produced by `PropertyHelpers.BuildGetter` already null-propagate through the internal backing property when the inner model is nullable - the change is purely to align the public API surface with the existing runtime behavior and with the ModelFactory signature.\\n\\n## Impact\\n\\nThis affects **every management RP** that:\\n- uses `properties?:` (optional inner, which is the common ARM pattern), AND\\n- has at least one required value-type (enum, `int`, `bool`, `DateTimeOffset`, closed struct-enum, ...) child inside `properties`, AND\\n- flattens `properties` (either implicitly via ARM resource templates or explicitly via `@flattenProperty`).\\n\\nIn the Compute migration alone this fix accounts for a large share of the remaining `ApiCompat MembersMustExist` errors (`OSType`, `OSState`, various `HyperVGeneration*`, sku-related flags, etc.). Fixing it in the generator avoids fanning out customization shims into every affected RP.\\n\\n## Repro / verification\\n\\nRun `Generate.ps1` in `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/` after the fix and diff the generated `CommunityGalleryImageData.cs` (or any test project that flattens a `properties?:` with required value-type children). The only expected changes are `T` → `T?` on the affected flattened properties.\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58288\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"ArcturusZhang\",\"id\":10554446,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/ArcturusZhang\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/10554446?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"Mgmt\"],\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-17T12:43:42Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-17T12:43:42Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}
2026-04-17T12:46:39.7958422Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-17T12:46:22.706153698Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"github","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"issue_number":58288,"method":"get","owner":"Azure","repo":"azure-sdk-for-net"},"name":"issue_read"}}}
2026-04-17T12:46:39.7987113Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-17T12:46:22.981978309Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58288,\"title\":\"[Mgmt Generator] PropertyFlatten drops Nullable wrapper on required value-type properties flattened from optional properties?: parent\",\"body\":\"## Problem\\n\\nIn [`FlattenPropertyVisitor.PropertyFlatten`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/Visitors/FlattenPropertyVisitor.cs#L650-L702), a required value-type property on an **optional** inner `properties?:` model is flattened as a **non-nullable** property on the outer model, which is unsafe (the getter will `NullReferenceException` if the inner `Properties` backing field is null) and inconsistent with the companion `SafeFlatten` code path, which correctly wraps such properties in `Nullable`.\\n\\n## Scenario (from Compute)\\n\\nTypeSpec:\\n\\n```tsp\\nmodel ProxyResourceCommunity extends PirCommunityGalleryResource {\\n  properties?: Properties;                     // OPTIONAL parent\\n}\\n\\nmodel CommunityGalleryImage\\n  is ProxyResourceCommunity { ... }\\n\\nmodel CommunityGalleryImageProperties {\\n  osType: OperatingSystemTypes;                // REQUIRED value-type child\\n  osState: OperatingSystemStateTypes;          // REQUIRED value-type child\\n  endOfLifeDate?: utcDateTime;                 // OPTIONAL child\\n  hyperVGeneration?: HyperVGeneration;         // OPTIONAL child\\n  ...\\n}\\n```\\n\\nGenerated (incorrect):\\n\\n```csharp\\npublic partial class CommunityGalleryImageData : ...\\n{\\n    internal CommunityGalleryImageProperties Properties { get; }   // may be null\\n\\n    // BUG: required value-type in optional parent - generated non-nullable, NRE risk\\n    public SupportedOperatingSystemType OSType =\\u0026gt; Properties.OSType;\\n    public OperatingSystemStateType OSState =\\u0026gt; Properties.OSState;\\n\\n    // OK: optional child - already nullable at the inner level\\n    public DateTimeOffset? EndOfLifeOn =\\u0026gt; Properties.EndOfLifeOn;\\n    public HyperVGeneration? HyperVGeneration =\\u0026gt; Properties.HyperVGeneration;\\n}\\n```\\n\\nExpected:\\n\\n```csharp\\npublic SupportedOperatingSystemType? OSType =\\u0026gt; Properties?.OSType;\\npublic OperatingSystemStateType?      OSState =\\u0026gt; Properties?.OSState;\\n```\\n\\nThis also matches the old AutoRest SDK\\u0026#39;s contract (e.g. `Azure.ResourceManager.Compute` historically exposed these as `Nullable`), so the bug is a breaking ApiCompat regression across every `properties?:` + required-value-type-child combination in the compute migration.\\n\\n## Root cause\\n\\n`PropertyFlatten` computes `isOverriddenValueType` but **never applies it** to the flattened property\\u0026#39;s type:\\n\\n```csharp\\n// FlattenPropertyVisitor.cs:650-702 (PropertyFlatten)\\nprivate void PropertyFlatten(ModelProvider model, ModelProvider propertyModel, ...)\\n{\\n    // dead: this tuple-list is declared but never populated\\n    var flattenedProperties = new List\\u0026lt;(bool IsOverriddenValueType, PropertyProvider FlattenedProperty)\\u0026gt;();\\n\\n    foreach (var innerProperty in innerProperties)\\n    {\\n        ...\\n        // computed but never consumed\\n        var isOverriddenValueType = innerProperty.Type.IsValueType \\u0026amp;\\u0026amp; !innerProperty.Type.IsNullable;\\n        var flattenedProperty =\\n            new FlattenedPropertyProvider(\\n                innerProperty.Description,\\n                innerProperty.Modifiers,\\n                innerProperty.Type,                  // \\u0026lt;-- BUG: should use WithNullable when isOverriddenValueType\\n                ...);\\n        ...\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\nCompare to the sibling `SafeFlatten` at [lines 739-744](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/Visitors/FlattenPropertyVisitor.cs#L739-L744), which applies it correctly:\\n\\n```csharp\\nvar isOverriddenValueType = innerProperty.Type.IsValueType \\u0026amp;\\u0026amp; !innerProperty.Type.IsNullable;\\nvar flattenedProperty =\\n    new FlattenedPropertyProvider(\\n        innerProperty.Description,\\n        innerProperty.Modifiers,\\n        isOverriddenValueType ? innerProperty.Type.WithNullable(true) : innerProperty.Type,\\n        ...);\\n```\\n\\nThe `IsOverriddenValueType` helper is also correctly consumed for **model factory method parameters** at [line 273](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/Visitors/FlattenPropertyVisitor.cs#L273):\\n\\n```csharp\\nif (IsOverriddenValueType(flattenedProperty))\\n{\\n    updatedParameter.Update(type: updatedParameter.Type.WithNullable(true));\\n}\\n```\\n\\nSo model factory parameters are generated as `Nullable` but the corresponding flattened property is generated as `T`. The types are internally inconsistent within a single generated model.\\n\\n## Proposed fix\\n\\nApply the same `WithNullable(true)` transform in `PropertyFlatten` that `SafeFlatten` already does:\\n\\n```diff\\n-                var isOverriddenValueType = innerProperty.Type.IsValueType \\u0026amp;\\u0026amp; !innerProperty.Type.IsNullable;\\n+                var isOverriddenValueType = innerProperty.Type.IsValueType \\u0026amp;\\u0026amp; !innerProperty.Type.IsNullable;\\n                 var flattenedProperty =\\n                     new FlattenedPropertyProvider(\\n                         innerProperty.Description,\\n                         innerProperty.Modifiers,\\n-                        innerProperty.Type,\\n+                        isOverriddenValueType ? innerProperty.Type.WithNullable(true) : innerProperty.Type,\\n                         flattenPropertyName,\\n                         ...);\\n```\\n\\nThe getter bodies produced by `PropertyHelpers.BuildGetter` already null-propagate through the internal backing property when the inner model is nullable - the change is purely to align the public API surface with the existing runtime behavior and with the ModelFactory signature.\\n\\n## Impact\\n\\nThis affects **every management RP** that:\\n- uses `properties?:` (optional inner, which is the common ARM pattern), AND\\n- has at least one required value-type (enum, `int`, `bool`, `DateTimeOffset`, closed struct-enum, ...) child inside `properties`, AND\\n- flattens `properties` (either implicitly via ARM resource templates or explicitly via `@flattenProperty`).\\n\\nIn the Compute migration alone this fix accounts for a large share of the remaining `ApiCompat MembersMustExist` errors (`OSType`, `OSState`, various `HyperVGeneration*`, sku-related flags, etc.). Fixing it in the generator avoids fanning out customization shims into every affected RP.\\n\\n## Repro / verification\\n\\nRun `Generate.ps1` in `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/` after the fix and diff the generated `CommunityGalleryImageData.cs` (or any test project that flattens a `properties?:` with required value-type children). The only expected changes are `T` → `T?` on the affected flattened properties.\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58288\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"ArcturusZhang\",\"id\":10554446,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/ArcturusZhang\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/10554446?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"Mgmt\"],\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-17T12:43:42Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-17T12:43:42Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}
